§ About

Hello.

BH
portrait — placeholder

I’m Brian. I lead accessibility at Superhuman, which some of you will still remember as Grammarly. Before that, I was a designer at Figma, an engineer at The Guardian, and — for a short, useful stretch — a two-person consultancy with a friend.

I’ve spent about equal time as a designer, a developer, and a manager of teams that include both. The longer I do this, the less those three labels feel like separate jobs — they feel like three rooms in the same house, with doors between them that I keep propped open.

Most of what I care about, professionally, comes back to one word: care. Care for the people using the software. Care for the engineers who have to maintain it. Care for the systems — the words, the components, the processes — that hold a product together when the humans are busy elsewhere.

This site is where I think in public. Essays are the long stuff; studies are the investigated stuff; notes are the unfinished stuff. Projects are what I’m actually building. The now page is the short version.

§ Work history

What I’ve done, in reverse.

  1. 2024 — present
    Remote

    Accessibility Lead

    Superhuman

    Following the Grammarly acquisition. Leading accessibility across the Superhuman surface: keyboard-first interaction model, screen-reader coverage, a cross-functional a11y council of 12. Shipping an annual external accessibility report.

    • Moved the flagship inbox from partial WCAG 2.1 AA to verified 2.2 AA across desktop and web
    • Hired and grew a dedicated a11y engineering partner
    • Published the "Accessible by default" internal playbook — now mandatory reading for new PMs
  2. 2020 — 2024
    San Francisco & remote

    Staff Designer → Accessibility Lead

    Grammarly

    Joined as a staff product designer on the writing assistant. Moved into an accessibility-lead role in 2022, partnering with engineering and research across five product surfaces.

    • Redesigned the suggestion card interaction for non-visual use — reduced average tab cost from 9 to 3
    • Ran a 14-person cross-functional council; shipped tokenised motion & contrast primitives
    • Coached six ICs into senior roles
  3. 2018 — 2020
    San Francisco

    Senior Designer, Platform

    Figma

    Platform design — plugins, the component system, and the library model. Quietly began the company's first accessibility working group.

    • Shipped the plugin-permissions model, still in use today
    • Founded an internal a11y working group with five volunteers
  4. 2016 — 2018
    London → SF

    Design + engineering consultancy

    Independent

    Two-person shop. Product design and frontend engineering for early-stage teams. Shipped eight products; half are still running.

    • Designed & built the initial web app for a healthtech startup now serving 2M users
    • Wrote a small design-system library that went on to have a surprisingly long tail on GitHub
  5. 2013 — 2016
    London

    Frontend Engineer, Interactive team

    The Guardian

    My first real job. Long-form interactive storytelling. Learned to ship on deadlines I did not set, for audiences that did not know I existed.

    • Built 30+ interactive features; two were nominated for a Malofiej
    • First introduction to accessibility, through a project with a blind sports correspondent
  6. 2012 — 2013
    Edinburgh

    MSc, Human-Computer Interaction

    University of Edinburgh

    Thesis on non-visual navigation of complex documents. Where I fell in love with the work I still do.

Elsewhere

  • GitHub github.com/brianhinton
  • Mastodon @brian@front-end.social
  • LinkedIn linkedin.com/in/brian-hinton
  • Speaking CSUN 2024, Config 2023, AxeCon 2022
  • Email hello@brian.is

— Typeset in Young Serif and Commissioner. Hand-built, maintained when there is time. The site is open-source; the source is here.

Design with Thought

A writing-and-projects site by Brian Hinton. Essays arrive slowly. Notes, more often.
